revision.comment: Auto-generated page
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Template:Selected biography/doc]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

revision.comment: /* Usage */ this was removed by [[User:Frietjes]] in July, but then put back in with my latest revert, so re-removing it now
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <includeonly>{{intricate template}}</includeonly> This template is used to configure the articles found in the '''''Selected person''''' or '''''Selected biography''''' section found on most Wikipedia Portal main pages. Use it on the article pages that are linked to the Selected person sub-page, e.g. '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected person/1'''. For the quickest and easiest way to create the '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected ''bar''''' sub-page and related article links, use one of the {{tl|Numbered subpages}} templates.
== Template layout == <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" width="75%"> {{Selected biography | image = | size = | side = | caption = | name = | born = <!--- Required if using "died" ---> | died = | text = <!--- Required ---> | link = <!--- Required ---> | page = | archive = }} </syntaxhighlight>
Additionally, the following templates can be used in the born and died fields:
For the '''born''' field: * {{tl|birth date}} * {{tl|birth date and age}} For the '''died''' field: * {{tl|death date}} * {{tl|death date and age}}
== Usage == It is unnecessary to include braces, quotation marks or other wiki-markup in the template. The '''''|text=''''' field does allow the use of wiki-markup at the contributor's discretion.
* '''image''' - Places an image relating to the subject of the article on the left side of the section. * '''size''' - Sets the image size, if left out or left blank it will default to 250px. * '''side''' - Set the location of the image here, it will default to left if no side is specified. Use right or center as the variable. * '''caption'''- Creates a caption for the image. * '''name''' - Adds the name of the person at the top left corner of the article, automatically bolded * '''born''' - Adds the person's birth date, format is at contributor's discretion. Requires the '''''name''''' field. * '''died''' - Adds the person's death date, format is at contributor's discretion. Requires the '''''born''''' and '''''name''''' fields. * '''text''' - A brief summary of the subject. * '''link''' - Sets the link in the '''Read more...''' that points to the full article. Required. * '''archive''' - Using this field with a value of ''no'' will remove the '''More selected biographies...''' link in the lower left corner.
== Examples == === Long form === The long form of this template places the subjects name immediately to the left of the portrait and gives you the option of adding birth and death years. If the {{para|born}} field is not included, the template will not include the {{para|died}} field.
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext"> {{Selected biography | image = Mark Twain, Brady-Handy photo portrait, Feb 7, 1871, cropped.jpg | size = | caption = Mark Twain, detail of photo by [[Mathew Brady]], February 7, 1871 | name = Samuel Langhorne Clemens | born = {{birth date|1835|11|30}} | died = {{death date and age|1910|4|21|1835|11|30}} | text = Samuel Langhorne Clemens, better known by the [[pen name]] '''Mark Twain''', ... | link = Mark Twain }} </syntaxhighlight>
Will yield this:
{{Selected biography | image = Mark Twain, Brady-Handy photo portrait, Feb 7, 1871, cropped.jpg | size = | caption = Mark Twain, detail of photo by [[Mathew Brady]], February 7, 1871 | name = Samuel Langhorne Clemens | born = {{birth date|1835|11|30}} | died = {{death date and age|1910|4|21|1835|11|30}} | text = Samuel Langhorne Clemens, better known by the [[pen name]] '''Mark Twain''', was an [[United States|A]]merican [[author]] and [[humorist]]. Twain is most noted for his novels ''[[Adventures of Huckleberry Finn]]'', which has since been called the [[Great American Novel]], During his lifetime, Twain became a friend to [[President of the United States|presidents]], artists, industrialists and European royalty.
Twain enjoyed immense public popularity, and his keen wit and incisive satire earned him praise from both critics and peers. American author [[William Faulkner]] called Twain "the father of [[American literature]]." | link = Mark Twain }}{{-}}
Because the {{para|name}} field formats the subject's name so that it is in bold text, it is suggested that you do not put the subject's name in bold in the text of the article.
=== Short form === <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext"> {{Selected biography | image = Mark Twain, Brady-Handy photo portrait, Feb 7, 1871, cropped.jpg | size = | side = right | caption = Mark Twain, detail of photo by [[Mathew Brady]], February 7, 1871 | text = '''Samuel Langhorne Clemens''', better known by the [[pen name]] '''Mark Twain''', ... | link = Mark Twain }} </syntaxhighlight>
Will yield this:
{{Selected biography | image = Mark Twain, Brady-Handy photo portrait, Feb 7, 1871, cropped.jpg | size = | side = right | caption = Mark Twain, detail of photo by [[Mathew Brady]], February 7, 1871 | text = '''Samuel Langhorne Clemens''', better known by the [[pen name]] '''Mark Twain''', was an [[United States|A]]merican [[author]] and [[humorist]]. Twain is most noted for his novels ''[[Adventures of Huckleberry Finn]]'', which has since been called the [[Great American Novel]], During his lifetime, Twain became a friend to [[President of the United States|presidents]], artists, industrialists and European royalty.
Twain enjoyed immense public popularity, and his keen wit and incisive satire earned him praise from both critics and peers. American author [[William Faulkner]] called Twain "the father of [[American literature]]." | link = Mark Twain }}{{-}}
== See also == {{Portal template list}}
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- ADD CATEGORIES BELOW THIS LINE --> [[Category:Wikipedia Portal templates|{{PAGENAME}}]]
}}</includeonly>

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: importing programmatically generated pages
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Template:Selected biography/doc]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: Mass importing wikipedia categories, not taking histories because these are unused ones and it is kind of obvious where they came from
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Template:Selected biography/doc]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]] to [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace {{subst:NAMESPACE}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: #[[en:Template:Selected biography/doc]] <noinclude></noinclude> <noinclude></noinclude><noinclude></noinclude> <noinclude> <noinclude></noinclude> </noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 1 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23582763848|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: #[[en:Template:Selected biography/doc]] <noinclude></noinclude> <noinclude></noinclude><noinclude></noinclude> <noinclude> <noinclude></noinclude> </noinclude> <noinclude> [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] </noinclude>

revision.comment: Text replacement - "</noinclude>↵<noinclude>" to ""
revision.text:
#[[en:Template:Selected biography/doc]] <noinclude></noinclude><noinclude> <noinclude></noinclude>
